defaultValue:
Any value below is merely an example for Modders. 
None of the Settings here do anything.

fieldDescription: Strings will accept any characters put in, with no filtering.
A button that switches between true and false.
Int Fields will only accept whole Numbers, and block any other character from being entered.
Doubles allow only numbers and a single decimal dot (.)

Lets users register a keybind. The saved keycodes are the same as used by org.lwjgl.input.Keyboard and Starsectors InputEventAPI.

Make sure to set a condition for your input checks to not accept numbers of 0, as lwjgl sets both unbound & unknown characters to that value.
Creates a color picker, giving both a hex text field and three sliders for the HSV color selection.

secondaryValue: None, Hegemony, Tritachyon, Persean League, Sindrian, Independent
fieldDescription:
Creates a radio button selector. 

The defaultValue decides which one is selected by default, the secondaryValue creates the list of choices with a comma seperated String. 
Can be retrieved as a String.
Enables additional Graphicslib effects for weapons, specifically extra lights and distortions.
Force sets the player to use from a random selection of unique portraits when using the [Real Human Being] Nexerelin start.

If enabled, the Pact begins the game with only a minority of their unique hulls and weapons, instead using an Independent-esque arsenal that largely adheres to their doctrine.

The Pact will gradually phase in new hulls and weapons to replace their common arsenal, and variants of their designs will proliferate to other factions in tandem. This progression can be influenced by the player and other factions.

When disabled, the Pact will use the equivalent of their final arsenal. If toggled mid-save, will require reloading the save to take effect, and will only affect newly spawned fleets & market inventories after restock.

This feature makes for a more spoiler-friendly experience for players newer to Starsector and better reflects the Pact's status as a recently formed polity, but may not be desireable if you wish to experience the full scope of this mod's content immediately.
Adjusts the baseline timespan over which the Pact's doctrine develops. Requires reloading the save to take effect.
Adjusts the initial progression the Pact has towards their finalized doctrine, as a percentage. Requires reloading the save to take effect.
Score requirement for the custom Nexerelin backgrounds added by this mod.

If using the [Real Human Being] start, completely removes the crew requirement for salvaging.  Takes effect when save is loaded.

This option exists to help make zero crew runs a little more viable - not an intended stipulation for this start, but one that can exist if you so desire it to.

fieldName: A Real Human Being of Humble Tastes
fieldDescription:
Enables the [Real Human Being of Humble Tastes] background, which combines the stipulations (and unlock requirements) of an older version of the [Real Human Being] background with the [Humble Tastes] background.  It's the whole pilot an AI ship deal, except you can only recruit Pact officers instead of having no officers.

In case you're looking to pilot a Glimmer for the entirety of your playthrough, you absolute psycho and also my kindred spirit.  This is effectively a scrapped prototype feature, so don't expect it to be well explained.
